response:
the interface as a way to share state and behaviour between objects that when first "constructed" did not seem to share a relationship. however, whenever i see architects design an application, the first thing they do is start programming to an interface. how come? how do you know all the relationships between objects that will occur within that interface? if you already know those relationships, then why not just extend an abstract class? include keywords: file, lack, path, salad.
